symptoms.symptom_name: Charred, white, leathery eschar over the central torso

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Charred leathery eschar is diagnostic of full-thickness burn depth.
A simple scald blisters and remains painful; it does not char.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of pain (analgesia) over the charred areas

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Destruction of dermal nociceptors renders full-thickness burns painless.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hoarse voice, throat burning and singed facial hair

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Voice change and singed facial hair after a confined-space fire are hallmark warning signs of inhalation airway injury.

symptoms.symptom_name: Inspiratory stridor and carbonaceous sputum in the oropharynx

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Stridor plus carbonaceous material confirms significant upper-airway inhalation injury — a clinician exam finding, sealed.
Objective distributive/hypovolaemic shock — a sealed clinician measurement; the AI Doctor infers circulatory compromise from the patient's dizziness and pallor, not a measured BP.

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Hypothermia from loss of skin barrier
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Evaporative heat loss through the burned surface; measured value is a sealed clinician finding.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hypoxia (SpO2 91% on room air) with tachypnoea (RR 32)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypoxia and rapid breathing reflect inhalation injury and possible carbon monoxide exposure; measured on a device the patient does not operate.

symptoms.symptom_name: Extensive burn surface area (~35% TBSA, anterior torso, both arms, face)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Formal TBSA estimation is a clinician task; magnitude drives fluid resuscitation and referral thresholds.

symptoms.symptom_name: Lightheadedness, feeling cold, pallor and sweating (patient-reported)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Patient-reportable proxy for circulatory compromise — the telehealth-accessible signal that shock is developing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Rapidly developing facial and body swelling

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Facial and airway oedema after a burn heralds worsening airway narrowing.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Inhalation injury with impending airway obstruction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Voice change plus stridor/carbonaceous sputum plus facial swelling after a confined-space fire predicts rapid airway occlusion and mandates emergency airway backup.
Large TBSA with hypotension, hypothermia and patient-reported dizziness defines a time-critical resuscitation need.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Formal in-hospital burns assessment (TBSA and depth mapping, airway evaluation)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive severity assessment and airway control require the burns unit / ED — not obtainable via telehealth; the AI's role is to expedite transfer, not to attempt assessment.
Confined-space fire raises carbon monoxide toxicity risk; a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth in real time.
